ruin_cluster.name: Hub_Cretaceous_Ruin_Cluster
ruin_cluster.description: Ancient stone ruin cluster at the main hub clearing. Weathered limestone pillars and fallen stone blocks overgrown with prehistoric ferns and cycads. Serves as a landmark and environmental storytelling element.

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_Pillar_001
actors.notes: Primary landmark pillar, tallest in cluster

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_Pillar_002
actors.notes: Secondary pillar, slightly offset for natural grouping

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_Pillar_003
actors.notes: Tertiary pillar, partially collapsed (use broken variant when available)

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_Pillar_004
actors.notes: Outer pillar, marks western edge of ruin cluster

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_FallenBlock_001
actors.notes: Fallen stone block, tilted to suggest collapse over centuries

actors.label: Ruin_Hub_FallenBlock_002
actors.notes: Second fallen block, creates natural cover geometry

atmospheric_lights.label: RuinLight_Hub_001
atmospheric_lights.notes: Warm amber shaft of light filtering through canopy near pillar 1

narrative_context.lore: These stone pillars predate human memory in the game world. Their origin is unknown — they may be natural formations shaped by ancient geological processes, or evidence of a civilization lost to deep time. The player can examine them for crafting material (stone chips) or use them as shelter from predators.
gameplay_uses: Landmark for navigation — visible from distance
Shelter geometry — player can hide behind fallen blocks from dinosaur AI
Crafting resource — stone chips from fallen blocks
Environmental storytelling — moss growth patterns indicate age and moisture
